key_points: Gopi represents kindness, imagination, and responsibility in the story
The magic brush symbolizes creative talent and the power to transform ideas into reality
The villagers represent society benefiting from generosity and helpful actions
The Zamindar represents greed, selfishness, and misuse of power
The poem emphasizes that creativity and moral courage can defeat greed and injustice
examples: Gopi paints useful items such as a plough and shawl to help villagers
The Zamindar demands treasures and a fortress to increase his wealth
Gopi paints a road and horse to escape from prison
She paints a river as a symbolic barrier to stop greed and injustice
The feast at the end represents celebration of kindness and community
formulas_or_processes: Symbolism → Magic brush represents imagination and responsible power
Climax → Creative problem-solving using painted objects
Resolution → Greed is defeated and kindness restores harmony
exam_tips: Explain the symbolic meaning of the magic brush in answers
Compare the characters of Gopi and the Zamindar when discussing themes
Highlight how creativity functions as a problem-solving tool in the story
State the deeper moral: talent and power should benefit society rather than personal greed
